conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 and still lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Are you responsible for Alex - meaning do you live with him and provide for his needs (like food, clothing, and care)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives here at home with me and I provide for all his needs - food, clothing, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions:

What is your current immigration status in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, do you have settled status, or do you have another type of visa or immigration permission?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen, I live here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Does anyone else also have Alex living with them, or is he living solely with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he lives solely with me. It's just the two of us at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in care (such as local authority care)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He's just at home with me, living a normal life - going to college and coming home every day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before giving you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I can now provide you with an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Although Alex is 18 (over the standard age of 16), you still qualify for Child Benefit because he is under 20 and in approved education (doing his A-levels at college). You also meet all the other requirements:

✓ You're responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you provide for his needs
✓ You're a UK citizen living in the UK
✓ Alex is not in hospital or care
✓ No one else is claiming for him

**Important note:** Your Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex stays in approved education or training and is under 20. If he finishes his A-levels or leaves education before turning 20, you may still be able to claim for up to 20 weeks if he registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services.

Also, be aware that if you or a partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (doing A-levels at college). According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, BUT you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is 18 and in approved education. Additionally, you are responsible for Alex - you live with him and provide for his needs (food, clothing, etc.), which satisfies the responsibility requirement. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, so you meet the residency requirements. Alex is not in hospital or care, and lives solely with you. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 18-year-old son Alex. Alex is still in approved education (doing A-levels at college), lives at home with the claimant who provides for all his needs, and is not in hospital or care. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for him.

Additionally, you are responsible for Alex - you live with him and provide for his needs (food, clothing, etc.), which satisfies the responsibility requirement. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, so you meet the residency requirements. Alex is not in hospital or care, and lives solely with you. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is still in approved education (doing A-levels at college), lives at home with the claimant who provides for all his needs, and is not in hospital or care. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for him.
